verdict_justification: The backend, orchestration, runtime, and deployment infrastructure are solid and production-ready. The frontend workspace is functional and proven. What is NOT ready for Prompt 3 is: (1) the frontend dashboard architecture lacks the sidebar/multi-page structure Prompt 3 requires, (2) Framer Motion is not installed for the animations Prompt 3 will add, (3) dedicated public website routes (/features, /pipeline, /access) don't exist, and (4) there is no CI/CD pipeline. These are all solvable in Prompt 3 itself — they are not blockers that prevent starting Prompt 3, but they define what Prompt 3 must deliver.

critical_blockers.title: No sidebar navigation / multi-page dashboard architecture
critical_blockers.description: The dashboard is a single /app page (ProjectList). Prompt 3 requires Dashboard Home, New Build, Workspace, Repo Workbench, Projects, Media Library, and Settings as navigable dashboard sections. The current monolithic layout cannot accommodate this without a structural refactor.
critical_blockers.impact: Prompt 3 cannot implement the required dashboard UX without this foundation
critical_blockers.fix: Create a DashboardLayout with sidebar, convert dialog-based features to routed pages, restructure /app/* routes

critical_blockers.title: No CI/CD GitHub Actions pipeline
critical_blockers.description: No .github/workflows/ directory exists. Zero automated test runs, zero deployment automation.
critical_blockers.impact: Code changes cannot be validated automatically; deployment requires manual intervention
critical_blockers.fix: Add GitHub Actions workflow: test (pytest + frontend build) + deploy (Docker Compose on VPS via SSH)

high_priority_blockers.title: Framer Motion not installed
high_priority_blockers.description: Framer Motion is not in frontend/package.json. Prompt 3 animation requirements (page transitions, micro-interactions, scroll-triggered animations) require Framer Motion.
high_priority_blockers.impact: Cannot implement Prompt 3 animation layer without installation

high_priority_blockers.title: No dedicated /features, /pipeline, /access routes
high_priority_blockers.description: These exist only as scroll anchors on the Landing page. Prompt 3 requires full dedicated pages.
high_priority_blockers.fix: Create pages/Features.jsx, pages/Pipeline.jsx, pages/Access.jsx with routing

high_priority_blockers.title: Preview auth token exposed in iframe URL
high_priority_blockers.description: Bearer token is appended to iframe src as a query param — visible in browser history, server logs, and referrer headers.
high_priority_blockers.fix: Implement short-lived signed preview tokens via /api/projects/:id/preview-token endpoint

high_priority_blockers.title: Playwright / axe-core not installed — real browser accessibility testing unavailable
high_priority_blockers.description: Accessibility agent uses regex-based static analysis only. Full browser-rendered WCAG testing requires Playwright + axe-core.
high_priority_blockers.impact: Cannot claim real accessibility scores — they are approximations
high_priority_blockers.fix: Add playwright + axe-core to requirements; provision headless Chromium in Docker

high_priority_blockers.title: Lighthouse not installed — no real Core Web Vitals
high_priority_blockers.description: SEO/performance agent uses static HTML analysis only. No real LCP, FID, CLS scores.
high_priority_blockers.impact: Performance scores are estimations, not real measurements
high_priority_blockers.fix: Integrate Lighthouse CLI or a Node.js sidecar service

medium_priority_blockers.title: Visual QA does not capture real screenshots
medium_priority_blockers.description: Visual QA agent analyzes LLM-generated code text, not actual rendered pages. No screenshot capture is implemented.
medium_priority_blockers.impact: Visual quality scores are LLM approximations, not real renders
medium_priority_blockers.fix: Integrate Playwright screenshot into PreviewService.build_preview(); pass screenshot to Visual QA

medium_priority_blockers.title: ExecutionGraph is a wrapper, not true DAG executor
medium_priority_blockers.description: The ExecutionGraph in app/orchestrator/execution_graph.py wraps the legacy Orchestrator and provides lifecycle tracking but delegates all execution to the linear pipeline. True parallel DAG execution is not implemented.
medium_priority_blockers.impact: Build pipeline remains linear — no parallelism, no true dynamic branching
medium_priority_blockers.fix: For Prompt 3+: implement per-node execution with asyncio.gather for independent nodes

medium_priority_blockers.title: Workspace not responsive for mobile
medium_priority_blockers.description: The multi-panel workspace layout is desktop-only. Mobile/tablet users cannot effectively use the workspace.
medium_priority_blockers.impact: Excludes mobile users from core functionality
medium_priority_blockers.fix: Responsive workspace: collapsible panels, mobile-first tab layout

medium_priority_blockers.title: No diff view for file changes between iterations
medium_priority_blockers.description: CodeViewer shows current file content only. Users cannot see what changed between iterations.
medium_priority_blockers.impact: Reduced transparency and trust in iteration system
medium_priority_blockers.fix: Add diff view to CodeViewer using version store API

medium_priority_blockers.title: test_result.md is empty (no test data recorded)
medium_priority_blockers.description: The test_result.md file contains only the testing protocol header — no actual test results have been recorded for this repo.
medium_priority_blockers.fix: Run backend/tests suite and record results in test_result.md

polish_items.title: Brave Search API gives Scout real web research capability — configure and document
polish_items.description: BRAVE_SEARCH_API_KEY is optional. Without it, Scout cannot search the web for competitor/market research.
polish_items.fix: Document in README and onboarding that Brave Search enhances Scout's research depth

polish_items.title: Dashboard /app URL is not intuitive — add /dashboard alias
No keyboard shortcuts in workspace
polish_items.description: Common actions (iterate, finalize, switch tabs) have no keyboard shortcuts

polish_items.title: ClarificationModal could be non-blocking
polish_items.description: Currently the modal must be dismissed before viewing anything — could be shown as a sidebar prompt

polish_items.title: test_reports/ and tests/ directories are placeholder/empty
polish_items.description: Top-level tests/__init__.py is empty; test_reports/pytest/ has only .gitkeep
polish_items.fix: Remove empty stubs or populate with actual test artifacts

future_items.title: True DAG parallel execution in ExecutionGraph
future_items.description: Implement per-node asyncio parallelism so independent agents run concurrently

future_items.title: Pricing / billing integration
future_items.description: quality_tier exists (cheap/balanced/premium) but no payment/subscription layer. Stripe is in requirements.txt but not wired to any endpoint.

future_items.title: Three.js / React Three Fiber in builder UI
future_items.description: 3D elements in the builder dashboard itself (not just in generated apps)

future_items.title: Real-time collaboration (multi-user workspace)
future_items.description: Multiple users editing/reviewing the same project simultaneously

future_items.title: Organization / team support
future_items.description: Currently only admin/user roles with no team or org concepts

backend_intelligence.notes: Full FastAPI backend, 18 agents, MongoDB, JWT, settings store — production-grade

agent_orchestration.notes: All 18 agents wired, ExecutionGraph tracking added, but linear pipeline only — no true DAG parallelism

runtime_previews.notes: SandboxManager + PreviewService implemented, honest results, but auth token in URL is a security gap

repo_repair.notes: RepairEngine with checkpoints, diffs, targeted fixes, PR creation — solid

versioning_rollback.notes: Full file snapshots, version records, restore API — production ready

media_logo_systems.notes: Media director, Pixabay, logo agent, persistent media library — comprehensive

realtime_websocket.notes: Hub with buffering, reconnect replay, MongoDB persistence, exponential backoff auto-reconnect

frontend_readiness.notes: Functional workspace but monolithic dashboard, no sidebar nav, Framer Motion missing, no dedicated public website routes

dashboard_readiness.notes: ProjectList serves as dashboard — no multi-page structure required for Prompt 3

public_website_readiness.notes: Landing page solid; /features, /pipeline, /access routes missing; Contact/Privacy/Terms exist

deployment_readiness.notes: Docker Compose, Nginx SSL config, go_live_check.sh, cleanup scripts — well prepared; missing CI/CD automation

qa_testing_readiness.notes: Backend test files exist but no CI runs them; no Playwright E2E; no GitHub Actions; Lighthouse/axe-core not installed

overall_average.notes: System is production-capable today for current feature set. Prompt 3 can proceed — it IS defining what needs to be built.

final_answer.explanation: The backend is solid. The WebSocket system is solid. The orchestration pipeline is solid. The deployment infrastructure is solid. What Prompt 3 will BUILD is the frontend/dashboard architecture that is currently missing — the sidebar, the dedicated pages, the animation layer, the public website routes. These are NOT pre-conditions to starting Prompt 3; they ARE Prompt 3. The only genuine pre-condition fix before Prompt 3 begins is: (1) install framer-motion (trivial, 1 command), and (2) acknowledge that CI/CD will be added as part of Prompt 3 delivery. Everything else is work for Prompt 3 to execute.
pre_prompt3_actions_required: Run: cd frontend && yarn add framer-motion
Run backend tests to establish baseline: cd backend && pytest
